params.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each appear. Full separation. Worked example designation visible.
Equation builder appears showing empty template: blank times blank.

beats.text: You've been matching expressions to equal groups. Now YOU get to build them, and you'll learn to go the other way too, figuring out what an expression looks like as a picture. Let me show you how building works.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each. Full separation. Worked example.
Equation builder showing empty template: blank times blank.

beats.text: First, I ask myself: how many groups? Each bag is one group.

params.description: Bags highlight sequentially as guide counts.

beats.text: 1, 2, 3, 4. Four bags. That's 4 groups.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each. All bags highlighted after sequential count.
Equation builder showing empty template.

beats.text: Now I ask: how many in each group? Let me look inside one bag.

params.description: First bag focuses, apples inside highlight.

beats.text: 1, 2, 3. Three apples. Let me check another.

elements.description: First bag focused with 3 apples highlighted inside.
Equation builder showing empty template.

params.description: Second bag highlights.

beats.text: Also 3. They're equal groups. Same number in every bag.

elements.description: Second bag highlighted showing 3 apples.
Equation builder showing empty template.

beats.text: So I have 4 groups of 3. Now watch how I build the expression.

params.label: groups
params.description: First slot glows with subtle label showing groups.

beats.text: The first number is how many groups. I click 4.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each visible.
Equation builder. First slot highlighted with groups label.

params.description: 4 appears in first slot. Guide demonstrates placement.

params.label: in each
params.description: Second slot glows with label showing in each.

beats.text: The second number is how many in each group. I click 3.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each visible.
Equation builder shows 4 in first slot. Second slot highlighted with in each label.

params.description: 3 appears in second slot. Complete expression shows 4 times 3.

beats.text: I built 4 times 3. This is called an expression. An expression uses numbers and symbols to show the math. No equal sign, just the numbers and the times symbol.

elements.description: 4 bags with 3 apples each visible.
Equation builder shows complete expression: 4 times 3.

incorrects.description: Student selected 6, confusing items per group with number of groups

params.description: Bags highlight to draw attention to containers as groups.

steps.text: The first number tells us how many groups. Each bag is one group. Count the bags.

params.description: Bags highlight one by one.

steps.text: We're not adding yet. Count how many bags you see. Each bag is one group.

params.description: Bags highlight to emphasize groups.

steps.text: You counted all the items. The question asks for groups. Count the bags, not the items inside.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Bags highlight one by one as guide counts.

steps.text: Let me show you. I count the bags: 1, 2, 3. Three bags means 3 groups. The number of groups is always how many containers you see.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3, confusing number of groups with items per group

steps.text: Look inside one bag. Count the items you see in that bag. How many are there?

params.description: First bag focuses with items inside highlighted.

steps.text: We're asking about one group. Pick any bag and count just the items inside that bag.

params.description: One bag highlights.

steps.text: You counted everything. The question asks how many in each group. Look at just one bag and count what's inside.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: First bag highlights, items inside pulse one by one.

steps.text: Here's how to find it. I look inside one bag and count: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. Six items in this bag. Every bag has the same amount, so each group has 6.

incorrects.description: Student selected 6 × 3, reversing the order of groups and items per group

params.description: Bags highlight one by one.

steps.text: The first number is groups. Count the bags. The second number is in each group. Count inside one bag.

incorrects.description: Student selected 3 + 6, using addition instead of multiplication

params.description: Multiplication symbol highlights.

steps.text: We need multiplication, not addition. Equal groups use the times symbol. How many groups times how many in each?

incorrects.description: Student selected 6 × 6, repeating one value instead of using both distinct values

params.description: Bags highlight, then items in one bag highlight.

steps.text: The two numbers are different. Count the bags for the first number. Count inside one bag for the second number.

incorrects.description: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Bags highlight sequentially, then items in one bag highlight sequentially.

steps.text: Let me walk through it. I count the bags: 1, 2, 3. That's my first number. Then I count inside one bag: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. That's my second number. The expression is 3 times 6.

steps.text: You found one equal group. Look closely at all the pictures. Are there any other bags where every single one has the same number inside?

steps.text: Equal groups means the SAME number in every bag. Look at the bags you selected. Do they all have the same amount inside? Count the items in each bag.

steps.text: You found at least one equal group. But check your other selections. Do ALL the bags in each picture have the same number inside? Count carefully.

incorrects.description: Heavy: Student answered incorrectly. System models the correct answer.

params.description: Option A animates: each bag highlights sequentially showing 4, 4, 4.
Option C animates: each bag highlights sequentially showing 3, 3, 3, 3.

steps.text: Let me show you. The first picture has 3 bags. Count: 4, 4, 4. Every bag has 4. That's equal groups, so we can write 3 times 4. The third picture has 4 bags. Count: 3, 3, 3, 3. Every bag has 3. Equal groups, so 4 times 3. The other two have different numbers in the bags. Unequal groups. No multiplication expression for those. The answers are A and C.

params.description: Equal groups visualization appears. 5 boxes with 2 items each. Concrete context showing boxes containing items.
Equation builder appears with two blank slots and multiplication symbol. Tile palette showing tiles 1 through 10 visible below.

beats.text: You've learned that you need equal groups to write multiplication expressions and that equal groups work the same way no matter what holds them. These are important ideas for multiplication.

elements.description: Equal groups visualization. 5 boxes with 2 items each. Reading mode, full separation, boxes_items context.
Equation builder with two blank slots. Tile palette 1-10 visible.

beats.text: You've been choosing complete expressions to represent equal groups. Now you'll build them piece by piece using number tiles. Watch.

elements.description: Equal groups visualization. 5 boxes with 2 items each. Reading mode, full separation, boxes_items context.
Equation builder with two blank slots. Tile palette 1-10 visible.

params.description: Boxes highlight one by one in sequence.

beats.text: Notice all of the groups are equal. Same questions: How many groups?
5 boxes. That's 5 groups. I need the 5 tile in the first slot.

First slot now filled with 5. Expression shows: 5 × [blank].

beats.text: I built it piece by piece. 5 times 2. Now you try with tiles.

beats.text: We've been going from pictures to expressions. Let's flip it around. If I see 2 times 6, what does that MEAN?

steps.text: Here's how this works. I count the circles: 1, 2. Two circles means 2 groups, so I place 2 first. Then I look inside one circle and count the dots: 1, 2, 3. Three dots in each group, so I place 3 second. The expression is 2 times 3. It shows 2 groups with 3 in each group.
